close_reason: Fixed by task agents - all changes committed and tests passing
description: The spec says id in subscribedResponse, unsubscribedResponse, and okResponse is optional (integer >=0). Our parse_incoming in protocol.rs:306 uses presence of id to distinguish Response vs Update. If the server sends a subscribed message without id, it would be incorrectly routed as an Update instead of a Response, breaking subscription tracking. Fix: Route messages by type field first (subscribed/unsubscribed/ok/error), then use id for correlation. Messages with these types should be treated as Responses regardless of whether id is present.
title: Handle optional id in subscribedResponse/unsubscribedResponse/okResponse

description: Many spec-required fields are Option: market_id, price_dollars, yes_bid_dollars, yes_ask_dollars, volume_fp, open_interest_fp, time. Extra non-spec fields: no_bid_dollars, yes_bid_size_fp, yes_ask_size_fp, bid_size_fp, ask_size_fp, last_trade_size_fp.
title: Fix TickerData requiredness and extra fields

description: Field name mismatches: yes_subtitle should be yes_sub_title, no_subtitle should be no_sub_title, rules should be rules_primary, expiration_ts should be expected_expiration_ts. Missing: rules_secondary, event_ticker, floor_strike, cap_strike, custom_strike.
title: Fix MarketAdditionalMetadata field names

Need to add build_list_subscriptions in protocol.rs, ListSubscriptions variant to StreamCommand, handler in session.rs, and public API on KalshiStreamHandle.
title: Implement list_subscriptions command

description: The spec allows send_initial_snapshot in update_subscription commands. The protocol builder build_update_subscription accepts it, but StreamCommand::UpdateSubscription doesn't carry it, and session.rs:654 hardcodes None. Fix: Add send_initial_snapshot field to StreamCommand::UpdateSubscription and pass it through to build_update_subscription.
title: Expose send_initial_snapshot for UpdateSubscription command

description: Spec says errors are {type: error, msg: {code, msg, market_ticker?, market_id?}}. Current parser looks for error: {code, message} or top-level code/message. Need to also handle the spec format where error details are inside msg. Also need to preserve market_ticker/market_id from error responses.
title: Fix error response parsing to match spec format

description: The event_lifecycle message type is completely missing from the implementation. Spec requires: event_ticker, title, subtitle, collateral_return_type (MECNET/DIRECNET/empty), series_ticker. Optional: strike_date (i64), strike_period (string). Need to add the struct, add it to StreamMessage enum, and handle it in from_type_and_value dispatch.
title: Add EventLifecycleData message type

close_reason: Fixed by task agents - all changes committed and tests passing
description: The seq (sequence number) is parsed in RawMessage as _seq (unused) in protocol.rs:218 and never included in IncomingMessage::Update. When building StreamUpdate in session.rs:929, seq is hardcoded to None. The spec defines seq as required on orderbook_snapshot, orderbook_delta, unsubscribed, ok, and order_group_updates. Consumers need this for gap detection on orderbook deltas. Fix: 1) Add seq to IncomingMessage::Update, 2) Parse _seq into it in parse_incoming, 3) Forward seq when constructing StreamUpdate in session.rs
title: Propagate seq field from wire protocol to StreamUpdate

title: Add typed enum for self_trade_prevention_type

description: The spec states all channels require authentication at the connection level. requires_auth() returns false for OrderbookDelta, Ticker, Trade, MarketLifecycle, Multivariate. This is semantically misleading - all channels need an authenticated WS connection. The distinction is private data channels vs public data channels. Fix: Rename to is_private() or requires_private_data() and update docs to clarify that auth is always required at connection level.
title: Rename Channel::requires_auth to requires_private_data or similar

description: The spec states ALL channels require an authenticated WebSocket connection. Currently requires_auth() returns false for OrderbookDelta, Ticker, Trade, MarketLifecycle, Multivariate. Should return true for all 10 channels.
title: Fix requires_auth() to return true for all channels

description:
Several DELETE endpoints return parsed response bodies but the API spec says they return 204 No Content:

- delete_api_key() returns DeleteApiKeyResponse but spec says 204 No Content
- cancel_rfq() returns RfqResponse but spec says 204 No Content  
- cancel_quote() returns QuoteResponse but spec says 204 No Content
- accept_quote() returns QuoteResponse but spec says 204 No Content

These should use http.delete() or http.put_no_content() instead of delete_with_response/put.

description: The spec defines error codes as integers 1-22 with specific meanings. Our IncomingMessage::Error::code and ChannelError::code are String. Numeric codes get converted to strings (16 -> '16'). This works but prevents typed error handling. Fix: Add a WsErrorCode enum with variants for all 22 spec-defined codes, use it in IncomingMessage::Error and ChannelError, with an Unknown(u16) fallback.
title: Add typed error code enum matching spec codes 1-22

description: The spec says okResponse.msg can contain both market_tickers (array of strings) and market_ids (array of UUIDs). The update_subscription response handler in session.rs:846-854 only extracts market_tickers. If a user subscribed via market IDs, the OK response would return an empty list. Fix: Also extract market_ids from the response msg and include them in the result.
title: Extract market_ids from okResponse in update_subscription handler
